about:
Jamie Strelec was born and raised in South Austin. She graduated from the University of Texas at Austin and, after a successful career in the corporate world, joined her family’s business, TexCen Realty, in 2009.

Jamie is a licensed Texas Broker Associate and plays a key leadership role at TexCen Realty as the Supervisor of the Property Management division and Office/Business Manager. In addition to overseeing operations and a property management portfolio, she is actively involved in residential sales—working with buyers, sellers, and investors to help them make smart, strategic real estate decisions. As a second-generation Realtor, she brings a well-rounded perspective that bridges both property management and sales.

She takes great pride in working alongside her family and being part of a business built on long-term relationships, trust, and service. If real estate ever feels overwhelming, she’s here to help make it simple, steady, and manageable.

Outside of work, Jamie enjoys the Austin scene—yes, even after living here her whole life. She’s always up for trying a new restaurant or revisiting an old favorite. She also deeply values time with her family and considers her close group of girlfriends her key to staying grounded (and sane!). If you’re looking for steady guidance and a real partner in your real estate journey, Jamie is always happy to help.

about:
Wishtan HomeServices is a full-service residential property management company headquartered in Macomb, Illinois, serving property owners and investors throughout West Central Illinois — including Macomb, Quincy, Galesburg, Carthage, and surrounding communities.

Founded in 2020, we manage single-family homes, multi-family properties, and student housing. Our clients include local owners and out-of-state investors who need a reliable, professional team managing their assets as if they were our own.

Our services cover the full management lifecycle: rental market analysis, professional marketing, thorough tenant screening, rent collection, maintenance coordination with in-house technicians, detailed financial reporting, and eviction protection. We operate on a structured business framework that prioritizes consistency, accountability, and measurable results.

Our track record speaks for itself:

— 90+% average occupancy rate across our portfolio

— 4.8+ star Google rating

— Lease agreements reviewed and approved by Illinois attorneys

— Full compliance with Illinois fair housing, habitability, and right-to-cure laws

We stand behind our placements. If a tenant we place doesn't fulfill at least 6 months of their lease, we find you a qualified replacement at no charge.

Wishtan HomeServices is not a side operation — property management is our sole focus. If you own investment property in West Central Illinois and want a management partner who operates with military-grade discipline and investor-level thinking, we'd welcome the conversation.

about:
My story starts in Georgetown, TX when I moved there in 2002, heeding the call of God (through government orders by the Army). At the time, I was a Military Intelligence officer coming back from serving almost 4 years in Germany with two deployments under my belt. I expected to finish off my commitment to the Army, marry my college sweetheart and then get a decent job wearing anything but the color green! However, God had different plans for me and a few months after marrying Autumn, I deployed with the 4th Infantry Division and my commitment to active duty was automatically extended. While sitting in one of Saddam Husseins bombed out palaces in Baghdad, God began to grow a desire in my heart to pursue a career in Real Estate. In October 2003, I finished my online classes and turned in my license for worldwide travel for a license to practice real estate in the state of Texas!

Six years after I was honorably discharged from the Army, I created my own real estate company, Mars Hill Realty Group. We now manage over 500 properties in Northern Virginia and Central Texas and would love to help partner with you!

property_types: Single Family, Multi Family, Commercial, Section 8, Retail
counties: Hunt, TX, Hopkins, TX, Rockwall, TX, Collin, TX, Fannin, TX, Delta, TX, Kaufman, TX
about:
I was born to a mechanic and software engineer in the small East Texas town of Cumby. I am the oldest son of three children who are still very close. After high school, I earned an HVAC certification from Paris Junior College and later graduated from Texas A&M-Commerce with a degree in Industrial Engineering. I then launched my professional career as a manufacturing engineer in Greenville Texas in 2013.

Married to a real estate aficionado, Ashley drug me into the real estate world when we bought our first project home in 2009. Even though all I knew about real estate when we married was that I like to sleep inside. It was soon clear with my engineering mind and love of the American Dream, our life would be driven by a passion for all things real estate. Committed to seeing my wifes dreams come true, I maintained my position as an engineer until the time was right.

In 2016 I left my engineering position after we purchased a portfolio of rentals. We had 6 construction projects going on our investment properties simultaneously. Ashley had signed 12 agents in the last year and was managing over 30 homes on her own while practicing as a top producing REALTOR. My goal was to immediately relieve her from as many administrative and construction/maintenance-related duties as possible in order to allow her to be the amazing broker I know she is. Licensed and serving next to my amazing wife I received my broker's license in 2021 and in 2022 Ashley and I both became Certified Real Estate Instructors to bring education to our agents.

Now, as the president of several growing real estate investment groups and partner in my wifes brokerage, I am devoted to helping bring home ownership and real estate investment to those who hunger for the dream!

TrueNest specializes in full-service residential property management, including tenant placement, rent collection, maintenance coordination, inspections, lease administration, and legal compliance.

about:
I am a real estate broker, investor, and property manager based in Central Ohio with extensive experience in residential real estate, investment properties, and property management operations. Over the years, I have built and managed a large portfolio of single-family and multi-family properties while working closely with property owners, tenants, contractors, and investors throughout the region.

I currently oversee the management of hundreds of rental units across Central Ohio, with a strong focus on Licking County and the surrounding communities. My experience includes leasing, acquisitions, renovations, maintenance coordination, tenant relations, and long-term investment strategy. Through a hands-on approach, I have developed systems and teams that support efficient operations, quality housing, and strong relationships with both owners and residents.

In addition to brokerage and property management, I am actively involved in real estate investing and redevelopment projects. I have extensive experience managing rehabs, coordinating contractors, improving rental properties, and identifying opportunities to increase property value and long-term cash flow. My practical knowledge of the market, combined with years of industry experience, has helped investors and property owners successfully grow and manage their real estate assets.

I am passionate about real estate and committed to providing professional service, maintaining quality housing, and helping clients navigate the ever-changing real estate market throughout Central Ohio.

about:
Matthew Nicklin is a Real Estate Broker and Investor. He started investing in Residential Real Estate in 2011 and has grown his portfolio from there. Matthew has helped many investors and owner-occupant home buyers find the right property for their needs. He has also helped many investors turn their property into a cash-flowing asset by taking over the property management and finding a quality tenant.

Matthew has invested in everything from buying and hold rentals, fix and flips, self storage, office, and commercial syndications. He has acquired these properties through retail sales, off-market sales, and foreclosures. He has experience in negotiating with retail sellers, distressed sellers, and financial institutions. He is very familiar with the foreclosure process in Georgia and the auction process on the courthouse steps.

Matthew attended Kennesaw State University and obtained a Bachelor’s in Business Administration with a concentration in Business Management. He began his Real Agent Career at EGA Realty, then went on to open his own Real Estate Brokerage OneSource Real Estate. Matthew is a NARPM PRO member in good standing.

about:
John Doss is the Co-Founder and BIC of Doss & Spaulding Properties LLC, serving investment property owners across Greensboro and greater triad. With a background as an active real estate investor with deep expertise in property management, John is recognized for his proactive service, transparent communication, and strategic focus on maximizing asset value.

John is an active board member of the Greensboro Landlord Association and a dedicated participant in NARPM. He is committed to upholding professional standards and fostering collaborative relationships within the property management community. Referring agents and property managers trust John for his integrity, responsiveness, and ability to deliver consistent results for both owners and tenants.

His management style is rooted in the core values of integrity, reliability, and transparency. John leverages advanced technology and streamlined processes to minimize risk and ensure seamless transitions for clients. He treats every property with the same care and diligence he would his own, making him a reliable resource for out-of-area managers and agents seeking a reputable partner in the Triad market.

Outside of property management, John is dedicated to health, wellness, and personal development. He sits on the board of the Greensboro Badminton Club and actively pursues fitness through badminton, pickleball, swimming, and strength training.

For property managers and agents seeking a referral partner who prioritizes communication, accountability, and client success, John Doss offers a proven track record and the highest standards of service.

about:
Meet Jeff and Jerry, twin brothers and business partners in property management. Their journey to success is a testament to their dedication and expertise. Jeff’s early start in real estate, obtaining his license at just 19, set the stage for their venture.

Meanwhile, Jerry joined the field in 2015. Together, they shared a common vision of assisting investors in building robust portfolios and ensuring that these investments yielded optimal returns. Their passion for providing exceptional service led them into property management in 2015.

In 2022, they took the leap and established Hornberger Property Management. Jeff and Jerry are committed to treating every property as if it were their own, underlining their unwavering dedication to their clients’ success.

Their combined experience and shared mindset make them a formidable team in the property management industry.

about:
Dan Baldini is an experienced property manager, entrepreneur, and investor with 29 years of experience in real estate. His focus is to help landlords and property managers optimize their residential portfolios for maximized earnings and financial freedom.

Known for his strategic vision and risk assessment approach, Dan excels at balancing operational excellence with tactical expertise that clients know they can trust. Dan passionately innovates the landlord experience from start to finish through detailed processes that safeguard investments, innovative solutions to maximize revenue opportunities, and comprehensive insight to predict and mitigate future problems.

Dan is a current member of the Metropolitan Indianapolis Board of REALTORS (MIBOR), the Indiana Association of REALTORS (IAR), the National Association of REALTORS (NAR), the National Association of Residential Property Managers (NARPM), a 4th Degree Member of The Knights of Columbus, a Life Member of the National Rifle Association (NRA), and an Eagle Scout.

A lifelong Hoosier, Dan earned a BS in Accounting from Indiana University Kelley School of Business and an MBA from Butler University Lacey School of Business. He enjoys matching up vacation travel destinations with his love of history, and currently lives in Indianapolis with his family.

about:
My name is Drick Ward. I am currently a licensed real estate broker in Southeast Virginia at Neptune Realty. I began as a real estate investor in 1987 and first became a licensed real estate salesperson in 1989. After working in the industry for a few years, I inactivated my license to work in IT management in the franchise sector and left the area in 2002. Throughout this time, I continued to build my portfolio and increase my training and education in real estate as a wealth building strategy.

In 2003, I returned to Virginia Beach and have been working as a real estate investment advisor, property manager, and am currently a broker associate with Neptune Realty. Why choose me to represent you as your property manager? My systems are designed with a best-in-class approach, assembling the pieces of technology necessary to ensure the investments of my clients perform as expected. My extensive knowledge of the Hampton Roads area and personal relationships with other professionals enable me to be much more effective than someone just starting out in Property Management. Because I limit the number of clients I will serve, you are assured your property will never get lost in the mix like it would at larger firms who are more interested in total door count than they are in exceptional client service.
My creative thinking and problem solving skills benefit everyone I represent to create a win:win.

about:
I have over 26 years of real estate sales and property management experience in Honolulu, Oahu, Hawaii.

Born in Agana, Guam, I was raised between California, Florida, and Hawaii. Most of my adult life has been spent living and working in Honolulu, Hawaii.

My career began in 1999 with commercial real estate broker, Colliers Monroe Friedlander. I transitioned to residential real estate in 2001 when I joined Prudential Locations. I actively engaged in real estate investing between 2000 and 2015, acquiring eight residential properties in Honolulu with a combined 14 rental units.

Taking advantage of a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, I worked as the Executive Vice President and eventually President & CEO of Century 21 Brazil Real Estate. In this role I was instrumental in introducing the iconic global brand to the Brazilian market, opening more than 120 independently owned and operated franchises in 20 states and the Federal District between 2008-2014. Much of my time in Brasil was spent implementing best practices and marketing techniques throughout our franchise system. During this time I learned Portuguese, married, and to this day speak both languages fluently, although my Portuguese is getting rusty.

They say you can take the boy from the island but not the island from the boy, and this was certainly true in my case. After seven years living between Sao Paulo and Honolulu, I grew homesick for our island lifestyle and working at the brokerage level, so I returned home in 2014, reactivated my Hawaii real estate brokers license (RB-18853), and began working as the Principal Broker of Hawaii Sands Realty in 2015.

about:
Carolyn Stewart is a full-time real estate professional dedicated to helping clients successfully achieve their real estate goals. Whether buying or selling a home, navigating short sales and foreclosures, managing probate transactions, completing BPOs, or overseeing property management, Carolyn brings extensive experience and a results-driven approach to every transaction.

With a strong foundation in property management, Carolyn managed a portfolio of 33 properties from 2005 to 2009. Her responsibilities included advertising available homes for tenant placement, conducting tenant screenings, issuing three-day notices, filing eviction cases and appearing in court for eviction proceedings, coordinating repairs and maintenance, and maintaining accurate bookkeeping while overseeing the daily operations of the properties.

Carolyn's professionalism, market knowledge, and proactive communication help ensure a smooth and informed experience for her clients from start to finish.

Before entering real estate, Carolyn built a successful background in sales, including car sales, advertising sales, and outside sales. She also served as a Supervisor for a Fortune 100 company, where she was recognized with the Excellence Award for her leadership and performance.

Carolyn Stewart is not only a REALTOR®, but also brings extensive industry expertise supported by several professional certifications, including PSA – Pricing Strategy Advisor, SFR – Short Sales and Foreclosure Resource, CPRES – Certified Probate Real Estate Specialist, C2EX – REALTORS® Commitment to Excellence, along with a Property Management Certification.

Outside of real estate, Carolyn is a devoted wife, mother of four, grandmother of fourteen, and great-grandmother of two who values family and community.

about:
Nancy Braun

Owner & Broker-in-Charge, Carolina Property Management, LLC | Charlotte, NC

With over 25 years of experience in real estate, property management, marketing, and leasing, Nancy Braun is a nationally recognized leader in the Carolinas. As the owner of Carolina Property Management, LLC, Nancy oversees a rapidly expanding portfolio of single-family homes, condos, and townhomes for individual investors, institutions, and corporate owners. Her forward-thinking approach blends technology-driven systems, aggressive marketing, and operational excellence to deliver unmatched results for property owners and tenants alike.

Results That Stand Out

  • Average Leasing Time: 10 Days — 26% faster than the competition.

  • Portfolio Growth — Trusted by both local owners and institutional investors across North and South Carolina.

  • High Retention & Satisfaction — Seamless communication, transparent processes, and consistent tenant care.

Technology-Driven Efficiency

Nancy leverages best-in-class platforms to streamline operations and elevate service:

  • AppFolio for real-time owner & tenant portals, online payments, and transparency.

  • Tenant Turner for 24/7 automated showing scheduling, maximizing exposure.

  • SentriLock for secure, trackable access — reducing vacancy and increasing showings.

Aggressive Multi-Channel Marketing

Every property benefits from Nancy’s proven marketing playbook:

  • Broad syndication across the MLS and top rental sites (Zillow, Realtor.com, Rent.com, Zumper, and more).

  • Professional photography, videography, and floor plans to drive quality leads.

  • Targeted email and agent campaigns for maximum visibility.

Leadership & Recognition

Carolina Property Management rated #1 for Charlotte and #20 for the Nation by independent audit: PropertyManagement.com. Nancy is not only a property management expert but also an award-winning real estate broker, national speaker, and coach. She has been recognized among the Top 1% of Agents in the U.S. by RealTrends & The Wall Street Journal and honored by FORTUNE and ICIC as one of the fastest-growing inner-city businesses in America.

Her mission is simple: to provide property owners with peace of mind, maximize returns, and deliver exceptional living experiences for tenants through innovation, integrity, and unmatched expertise. A+ BBB rating

about:
Working in residential real estate for over 20 years, I have often found myself representing tenants who are lost and exhausted. Trying to set up showings on rental properties and getting no response from listing agents; combing through the MLS and seeing page after page of poorly marketed listings and wrong or incomplete information; discovering loads of inconsistencies in the application and leasing process - it is not hard to imagine how overwhelming the process must feel to a prospective tenant, especially those that are relocating from out of the area.

On the landlord side, I have seen countless property owners failing to maximize their investments - securing leases at below market rental rates, having to trudge through the eviction process for lease violations and non-payment of rent and tired of answering the phone at all hours for repair requests. I often get calls from investors with underperforming properties they are just looking to offload because they are tired of the headaches.

I KNEW THERE WAS A BETTER WAY.

The idea to open a property management division was not a new one. Over the last few years since I have been back in the DC area, I have executed over 100 leases - I was shocked when I actually pulled a report! I have always helped my past clients run an analysis when they are considering selling a property to determine if perhaps holding onto it as a rental makes sense. I have made a lot of property owners first-time landlords and walked them through the process of self-management, property marketing and tenant procurement. And I was always the one that got the calls with questions for recommended vendors and solutions to common landlord issues. It seemed like a natural progression to create a full-service property management company. I am excited to serve my clients at an even higher level.

GET TO KNOW ME…

I began my real estate career in 2001 working for a production builder. One could say I fell into real estate completely by accident and learned to embrace it! I have worked in Austin and Dallas, Texas, for several years, but got licensed in DC, Maryland and Virginia in 2005. Cumulatively, I have now been in the DC Metro area for over half my career. I have my Associate Broker’s license with eXp Realty, am a Certified Residential Specialist and a Military Relocation Professional.

Aside from my real estate career, I wear a few other hats - baseball wife, girl mom, dog lover, community resource and philanthropist. My husband Andrew and I live in Rockville and own an investment condo in North Bethesda. When I’m not slinging houses, you can probably find us cheering on our favorite sports teams in the DMV (go Nationals, Capitals and DC United - we can just agree to disagree on football), volunteering with a local nonprofit organization or checking out a new restaurant! about:
Im Sherry J. Oka, a dedicated real estate professional committed to delivering exceptional results, personalized service, and unwavering integrity. With over twenty-five years of industry experience and a real estate license Ive held since 1999, Ive earned several respected credentials, including Certified Property Manager (CPM), Certified Real Estate Brokerage Manager (CRB), Seniors Real Estate Specialist (SRES), and Military Relocation Professional (MRP).

Over the years, Ive had the privilege of assisting military personnel and their families, originally near Fort Benning, Georgia, and I played a key role in managing large rental property portfolios, most notably, the privatization of over 1,400 homes at Iroquois Point Island Club. I hold an Associate of Science degree in Paralegal Studies and gained valuable insight during my time at a Honolulu-based real estate litigation firm. In 2020, I earned my Brokers license to further enhance the service I provide to my clients.

My passion lies in helping people navigate the complexities of buying, selling, and managing investment properties with confidence. Im an active member of the Honolulu Board of Realtors, the Institute of Real Estate Management (IREM), and the National Association of Residential Property Managers (NARPM), where I stay engaged and continue to grow within the profession.

When Im not working, youll often find me hiking, enjoying water sports, or deep-sea fishing. I also make time to give back through volunteer work, supporting families and housing initiatives.

As both an investor and property manager, Mike built Arpent with a simple mission: create a property management company designed by investors, for investors. The company specializes in managing single-family homes and small multifamily properties, with a focus on maximizing returns while protecting long-term asset value.

Whether you’re a first-time landlord or a seasoned investor, Mike and the Arpent team are committed to helping you scale and manage your portfolio with confidence.

about:
Property Management Inc. of Tree City is a full-service residential property management company serving the Treasure Valley. We specialize exclusively in long-term and mid-term residential rentals, allowing us to provide focused, compliant, and performance-driven management for real estate investors and homeowners.

Our approach is built on clear communication, strong systems, and proactive asset protection. From pricing strategy and tenant placement to ongoing management, inspections, and financial reporting, we operate with the mindset that each property is a long-term investment—not just a transaction. We emphasize risk mitigation, consistent oversight, and transparency so owners always know how their property is performing.

As a locally owned and operated firm backed by the national Property Management Inc. network, we combine local market expertise with proven processes and technology. We do not manage short-term or vacation rentals, which allows our team to stay highly focused on tenant quality, lease compliance, maintenance coordination, and owner profitability.

Property Management Inc. of Tree City is committed to professional standards, continuing education, and ethical property management. Our mission is simple: protect the asset, reduce owner stress, and deliver consistent results.

about:
I've been living and working in the Greater Phoenix/Scottsdale area since 1982. I've been a full time Realtor in Arizona since 1995.
With a previous career in Commercial Design, I have combined both my skill and knowledge of the real estate market and my background in design. This affords me the opportunity to not only help my clients in understanding the benefit of sprucing up the property they want to sell but also what upgrades/remodel ideas they might consider when buying. I am able to consult both on value and cost.
In the last three years I have been spending more time building my long standing Property Management business and welcome referrals. When receiving a Landlord with property to be leased and/or managed, I always return that landlord to the referring agent when they mention they're thinking about buying or selling.
My business is based on both Referral and Repeat clients.
I work with Buyers and Sellers, help investors to make the right choices and place renters and manage those investment (rental) homes. I am a Certified Residential Property Manager (CRPM)and Certified Risk Management Specialist (CRMS).
My priority is to always listen to what my client says to that I am able to deliver what they want.
My skills include negotiation and constant research into the market. I have an understanding of Areas, Builders, Floor Plans, Value and Pricing to always provide value to my clients by not only providing the knowledge and information to make informed decisions, I also Promote and Protect them by always placing their interests first.

about:
Ed Drummond is the Owner and Designated Broker of Capstone Realty Professionals, a full-service residential real estate and property management brokerage serving the Phoenix metropolitan area. Since 2007, Ed has helped more than a thousand buyers, sellers, and investors successfully navigate the Valley market, building a reputation rooted in transparency, accountability, and long-term relationships.

After years in the industry, Ed recognized that real estate done well is never a solo act. When Capstone was founded in 2012, the vision was clear: build a collaborative team environment that delivers a higher level of service than any single agent could provide alone About Us - PM. Today, that vision has grown into a dedicated team managing more than 600 single-family homes and serving hundreds of clients across the Valley.

Under Ed’s leadership, Capstone operates as a true full-service brokerage—supporting clients in buying, selling, leasing, and managing residential real estate Why Capstone Is AWESOME. The firm’s management platform includes tenant placement, screening, maintenance coordination, lease enforcement, accounting, and 24/7 support Management Services, all delivered with a clear commitment to transparent pricing and no hidden fees 2025 Management Costs (2).

Ed is a firm believer that relationships are more valuable than properties—a philosophy that drives Capstone’s team-based approach and long-standing client retention Capstone RESALE Listing Present…. He is also active in the professional community and leads Capstone’s annual “Capstone Contributes” initiative, supporting local outreach efforts and reinforcing the company’s belief that strong communities build strong portfolios.

Focused on growth, operational excellence, and raising the standard of service in property management, Ed continues to challenge his team—and the industry—to think bigger, communicate clearly, and treat every home as if it were their own investment.

about:
Jon Beaman, Broker and Owner of Traverse Property Management, has been a licensed Realtor since September 2013 and a Broker since April 2023. Jon started with a large corporate property management company in January 2014 and decided after 7 years of experience in residential property management he wanted to break away from the corporate side of the business. Jon saw a huge need for improved communication between landlords and tenants. Jon decided it was time to get involved with a higher personal touch to provide a superior quality of service hence Traverse Property Management. I want to bring the property management industry to a new level when it comes to keeping fees at a minimum for tenants, increasing overall retention and reducing maintenance cost/markups for owners to maximize their returns. This mindset is what sets Traverse Property Management apart from our competitors.

In addition, Jon remains current with the National Association of Residential Property Managers (NARPM) chapters and local Realtors. Jon places a strong focus on customer service, as it has been at the core of our company and drives our success. Jon previously served as Director of Sales (2017-2021), Quality Assurance Manager (2015-2017) with an emphasis on business reputation management, responding to reviews placed by the consumer, and as a leasing agent serving the DFW region (2013-2015) for Specialized Property Management.

Prior to entering real estate, he worked as survey crew chief for eleven years for Gorrondona & Associates, and was previously employed at Byrn & Associates Surveying and Engineering, both companies working in civil development and working closely with water districts, municipalities, and the Texas Department of Transportation. about:
John is Owner and CEO of Tiner Properties – a full-service residential property management and real estate sales company.

Tiner Properties is a 3-generation family business started by his parents in 1976.

John joined the family business in 1987 and has grown the company from 40 rentals to well over 1350 currently. John has earned the designation of GRI (Graduate of Real Estate Institute) and is an Outstanding Life Member of the Masters Club.

With nearly 4 decades of experience, John has become an expert in all aspects of property management, investment properties, and real estate sales. John regularly testifies as an expert witness in court and is a regular guest speaker for Realtor and Investor groups, sharing his expertise with other Real Estate professionals. In addition, he has been a continuing member of NARPM (National Association of Residential Property Managers) since 1994.

As CEO, John maintains the Tiner culture of respect, professionalism, and excellence within his team, signs up new accounts, and sells investment real estate. He spends considerable time reading legal blogs and developing best practices for the management team. 

John especially appreciates working with his parents and his wife, Jenny, in the family business. When he is not working, he enjoys traveling, spending time with his grandchildren and twin stepsons, woodworking, and playing racquetball & pickleball.

Hal Wilkerson, RB-24258 | Principal Broker & CEO Hawaii Coastal Property Management (HCPM) Honolulu, Hawaii

Hal Wilkerson brings a U.S. Navy veteran's discipline, and mission-first mindset to residential and commercial property management across Oahu. After years of military service, he founded Hawaii Coastal Property Management with a simple operating principle: treat every owner's asset like it's your own.

As a board member of the NARPM Hawaii Chapter, Hal is deeply invested in elevating professional standards across the islands — authoring the monthly Hawaii PM Pulse newsletter, tracking legislative developments affecting landlords and managers, and fostering collaboration among Hawaii's property management community.

HCPM leverages a modern, tech-forward stack — including AppFolio, Hostaway, and PriceLabs — to deliver transparent reporting, dynamic pricing, and responsive operations at scale. The firm manages a diversified portfolio spanning long-term rentals, mid-term/corporate housing, short-term vacation rentals, commercial properties, and HOA/site management contracts.

Whether you're a first-time investor or a seasoned portfolio owner, Hal and the HCPM team offer the local expertise, systems, and accountability to protect and grow your investment in one of the nation's most dynamic markets.

We are an established Illinois licensed brokerage firm and have specialized in professional property management for nearly 30 years. Property owners choose Christone Enterprises for the security of knowing we only place quality tenants in their properties. Similar to the Special Forces, Team Harris Real Estate (est. 1992) is organized on a horizontal leadership model that expects each team member to take ownership and action in order to best accomplish our mission and aid our clients. This model works exceptionally well in our small culture-driven company where confidence and trust in each other's abilities and objectives is paramount. We look forward to being our clients' life-long REALTOR resource no matter if the needs are planned or unexpected.

We are here for clients needing existing homes, new construction, relocation expertise, property management, land investment or divestment, property investing, upsizing or downsizing, aging in place, estates and probate. Our mission is to:

Advocate. Educate. Innovate.

Wendy Harris is recognized as an experienced voice of real estate with more than twenty years in the industry and area. Her market knowledge, client loyalty and transaction management skills set her apart. With a belief in the possible, she is undaunted by the constant changing dynamics of the real estate industry and dedicates herself to her clients selling, buying and investing decisions.

As a second-generation REALTOR, army brat and dual citizen, Wendy spent her childhood relocating, and carried this into adulthood as she explored her career options. Having lived and worked in three continents, five countries, seven states, and a sailboat, she has built upon her personal and professional experiences. All of this and the fortune of being a second generation Realtor and daughter of a retired Army officer, is put to use as a valuable resource for clients. about: Chato Castillo is the Broker/Owner of Pioneer 1 Realty, Inc. that provides property management services. He has been doing this since the year 2000 and currently has hundreds of properties in his property management portfolio. Half of his portfolio are overseas investors from Australia, Japan, Canada, Egypt, Singapore and Hong Kong. counties: Alameda, CA, Contra Costa, CA
about:
Jason Crouch is the founder of All East Bay Properties, which he established in Emeryville in 2005. For more than 20 years, he has managed residential rental properties across Oakland, Berkeley, Emeryville, and the broader East Bay — navigating some of California’s most tenant-protective rental markets in the country.

Jason holds a California real estate broker license (DRE #01295378) and is a member of the Bridge Association of Realtors. He has served as Chair of the Emeryville Chamber of Commerce, as incoming Chair of the Oakland Association of Realtors, and on the board of BridgeMLS. He was also a board member of ECAP, the Emeryville Citizens Assistance Program.

All East Bay Properties is a boutique, owner-operated firm proudly managing 600+ rental units across Emeryville, Oakland, Berkeley, Richmond, El Cerrito, Pinole, Alameda, San Leandro, Albany, Kensington & the greater East Bay since 2005.

Led by Broker/Owner Jason Crouch (CalDRE #01516255), we’re East Bay landlords ourselves—we protect your investment like family.

WHY 300+ OWNERS TRUST US

• Lowest fees: 6–8% management + ½ month leasing

• ZERO setup or cancellation fees • True month-to-month (30-day notice)

• AI leasing with LISA™: 24/7 auto-response, pre-screening & scheduling in AppFolio

• Listings on 30+ sites + pro photos & 7-day showings

• Full compliance: Oakland RAP, Berkeley Measure BB, AB 1482, SB 567, Section 8

• 24/7 maintenance (English/Spanish) • $300 minor-repair auth • NO vendor markups

• ACH payouts within 7 days • Real-time AppFolio portal with 1099s

• Quarterly inspections, renewals, evictions & 21-day deposit returns

Perfect for out-of-state, overseas, trust/probate & first-time owners—no 3 a.m. calls.

Ready for higher NOI & zero headaches?

📞 510-450-3800

📧 pm@alleastbayproperties.com

🌐 alleastbayproperties.com/property-management-services

📍 2324 Powell St, Emeryville, CA 94608

Follow @AllEastBay on social media for articles, tips, trivia and more...

about:
Patricia has been with Property Professionals and a part of the New Braunfels community since 2006. In addition to serving as the companys VP of Operations, Patricia also oversees the management of our many property owner associations.

Staying informed and current with our industries rules and regulations is very important to Patricia, therefore she always takes an opportunity to continue her education. She has earned her CRB (Certified Real Estate Brokerage Manager), GRI (Graduate, REALTOR Institute), SRS (Sellers Representative Specialist), TRPM (Texas Residential Property Manager) and TRLS (Texas Residential Leasing Specialist) designations. She has also completed the ABR (Accredited Buyers Representative) courses.

Patricia is a member of NARPM (National Association of Residential Property Managers) and CAI (Community Associations Institute). These associations help members stay current with the laws that affect property management and property owner associations.

Patricia is always happy to help and answer any questions or concerns. She also speaks Spanish.

about:
Lisa Stiver joined the real estate industry while living her passion of assisting clients with their second homes in Arizona. As the owner of Dreamweaver Concierge, Lisa worked at all hours of the day and night to safeguard her client's homes while taking care of them, their families and their guests. She coordinates every aspect of her client's needs while ensuring they relax and enjoy their homes. Her success in this role made for a seamless transition to real estate, where she can apply her meticulous attention to detail and communication prowess every day.
Throughout her career, Lisa has always been passionate about helping people. The personal fulfillment she gets from guiding clients through what is often one of their most significant life transitions drives her forward. With Lisa as your agent, you can expect persistence, unwavering support, and, of course, fun! She puts in the hard work so that you can sit back and enjoy the process and success.
Outside of real estate, you might find Lisa enjoying her family and four Schnauzers. Shes a self-proclaimed foodie and family girl whos happy to be settled in Arizona after being born and raised in New Mexico. She would love to introduce you to some of the best Mexican restaurants in your neighborhood.

about:

I manage both furnished and unfurnished rentals and work closely with owners to create management solutions that fit their goals. From tenant and guest communication to maintenance coordination and property oversight, I take a proactive approach to every property I manage.

about:
Renee Morris
Vice President of Property Management
Atlanta Fine Homes Sotheby’s International Realty

Renee Morris serves as Vice President of Property Management at Atlanta Fine Homes Sotheby’s International Realty, where she leads the firm’s property management division with a focus on delivering a refined, full-service experience aligned with the Sotheby’s International Realty brand.

With over 20 years of real estate experience in Metro Atlanta, Renee began her career in 2002 as a Realtor specializing in both resale and new home construction. In 2016, she transitioned into property management leadership, serving as Director of Property Management at BHHS Georgia Properties through 2025. In that role, she oversaw portfolio growth, operational performance, and elevated client service delivery across a large-scale portfolio.

At Atlanta Fine Homes Sotheby’s International Realty, Renee has positioned property management as a natural extension of the sales platform—designed to support the full client lifecycle from acquisition to leasing, management, and resale. Her approach centers on protecting client assets, elevating property standards, and delivering a high-touch, professionally managed experience for both owners and residents.

Known for her thoughtful leadership and detail-oriented execution, Renee is committed to raising the standard of property management within the luxury space while fostering strong relationships with advisors, clients, and industry partners.

about:
Since 1980, Mr. Ellis has been successfully involved in the operation of apartments and other residential rental real estate, handling a portfolio as large as 6,000 apartment units in four major markets.

In 1984, Mr. Ellis began directing his efforts primarily to the individual investor/owner. His objective is to provide the same level of expertise and experience to the single family and duplex investor that is often offered only to large multi-family portfolios.

Besides managing real estate, Rick is recognized as an industry expert in apartment marketing, property positioning, and the evaluation of rental markets and submarkets throughout the nation. Mr. Ellis is a much sought after speaker, making hundreds of presentations each year on topics from basic real estate management and resident retention to the economics of apartments.

Mr. Ellis received a Bachelor of Science in 1977 and Masters of Art in 1979 from Abilene Christian University, Abilene, Texas. He holds the Certified Property Manager (CPM) designation from the Institute of Real Estate Management. He also holds a Texas Real Estate Broker license and is an Eagle Scout.

Rick and his wife Diane live in Coppell, TX with their two King Charles Cavalier Spaniels, Belle, and Mac. They have 4 adult children who are completely off the family payroll. Rick and Diane are active in their church, love to travel, and hang out on their back porch. In the summer they live in Clarence Center NY to duck the HOT Texas summers.

about: Waypoint Residential Management was started by Shannyn Flory, whose vast experience in the real estate industry began in 1992 and has encompassed the areas of title and escrow, lending, home appraising, investing, property management and sales. In 2003, after several years as a property manager, she created an electronically formatted How-To guide to property management and launched The Complete Landlord E-Guide along with an interactive companion website called The Landlords Resource Directory. Her experience led her into work as a Landlord Coach and accounting consultant for other property managers. Years later, after much success is real estate sales, the need for a new type of property management service emerged in the wake of the real estate crash. The sophisticated brand strategy for Waypoint Residential Management was developed by Shannyn in 2009 to assist property owners looking to hold on to their homes until the real estate market could recover. Shannyns unique mission and commitment focuses on a premium level of customer service, catering to the specialized needs of high end residential properties and implementing management strategies for maximum property maintenance as a means to maintain property values. Shannyn also perceives and nurtures what she feels is an essential and natural partnership with the real estate sales community to exchange high quality referrals and provide interim leasing solutions in a continually changing housing environment.

about:
Tim Sharkey brings extensive experience in residential and commercial property management, including working with large institutional investors and corporate real estate clients. His expertise includes managing diverse property portfolios, optimizing operations, and enhancing profitability for single-family and multifamily properties. Known for delivering tailored solutions, Tim focuses on helping property owners achieve their investment goals while maximizing asset performance.Tim Sharkey is a seasoned real estate investment and property management professional with more than two decades of experience across residential, multifamily, and commercial asset classes. He has worked with a broad range of clients, from individual investors to large institutional and corporate portfolios, delivering operationally sound management strategies that drive measurable performance and long-term asset value.

Tim's background spans the full property lifecycle, including acquisitions, leasing, tenant relations, maintenance operations, and portfolio optimization. He brings a hands-on, data-driven approach to every engagement, with a track record of reducing operational inefficiencies, improving net operating income, and scaling management systems that support growth without sacrificing owner experience.

Beyond traditional long-term management, Tim has deep expertise in short-term and mid-term rental operations, corporate housing, and the strategic positioning of properties across multiple revenue channels. His work with investors extends into acquisition advisory, market analysis, and exit strategy, giving clients a full-service partner who understands both the operational and investment sides of real estate.

Tim is committed to transparent communication, accountability, and results. Whether working with a single-asset owner or a multi-property portfolio, he brings the same level of rigor and professionalism that has defined his career across Metro Atlanta and the broader Southeast market.

about:
I’m Donovan Cobb, owner of PMI BeltLine in Atlanta. Real estate has been part of my journey since I bought my first property at 21. I became a licensed real estate agent in 2007 and earned my broker’s license in 2015. Along the way, I’ve built my rental portfolio and worked with property managers both locally and out of state, giving me firsthand insight into the challenges property owners face.

With a background in Computer Information Systems, I bring a unique ability to blend technology with personal service. At PMI BeltLine, we combine the structure and technology of PMI with a hands-on, problem-solving approach that keeps communication clear and service top priority. My team and I focus on solving issues before they become headaches, caring for each property like our own, and creating win-win-win solutions for owners, tenants, and vendors.

Clear communication. Creative solutions. Complete peace of mind.
From the Atlanta BeltLine to the 'Burbs we have you covered!

about:
Dawn Josemans is the Directory of Property Management, managing several hundred units for Coldwell Banker Elite. Dawn is also an active licensed REALTOR busy listing and selling homes.

Dawn serves on the Virginia Association of Realtors Property Management Committee and holds the following NAR designations: ABR, CDPE, SFR.

Dawn has been managing Real Estate since 1989. She has worked for some of the largest property management firms in the country. Dawn has an incredible work ethic and her success speaks volumes about her integrity and commitment to her clients and co-workers. Dawn is dedicated to continually educating herself and making sure she up on the latest trends, market changes and new industry laws & regulations.

On a personal note Dawn enjoys spending time with her family, especially her grandchildren and being outdoors. Dawn most particularly enjoys fishing, scuba diving and being on the water.

about:
True Home Ohio Property Management is a property management focused brokerage serving rental property owners throughout Cincinnati, Dayton, and the broader Southwest Ohio market.

We manage single family homes, duplexes, small multifamily properties, and scattered site rental portfolios for owners who want real systems, real communication, and a company that understands rental property from an investor’s perspective.  We are not a "one-man show" type of operation - we have systems, processes & procedures, and believe we are the most sophisticated property management company in Southwest Ohio.

Unlike many brokerages, property management is not a side business for us.  It is the business.  Our team handles leasing, tenant screening, rent collection, maintenance coordination, renewals, inspections, owner communication, and monthly reporting through professional systems built for scale.

Our owners get access to transparent portals, documented maintenance workflows, 24/7 emergency maintenance support, detailed screening standards, aggressive rent collection processes, and practical guidance on pricing, leasing, and long term property performance.

If you are looking for a company that will simply “collect rent,” we are probably not the right fit.  If you want a professional management partner focused on protecting your asset, improving performance, and reducing the headaches of rental ownership, True Home Ohio Property Management is built for that.

about:
I am originally from Phoenix, AZ but have been in Tucson the majority of the time. I am a single mother of three boys and I currently have three dogs. I have worked in various aspects of real estate since 2001. I have experience in such roles as title secretary, customer service coordinator for a builder, customer service manager for a builder, a marketing/salesperson for another real estate brokerage, and a property manager for 18+ years. Although I enjoy property management, the brokerage I was working for did not do sales at all. So, I decided to leave that position to open my own brokerage so that I could do both sales and property management. I opened Mac's Pro RE in October of 2020 - during the worst of the worst - the Covid pandemic.

In working in management, I found a desperate need to have a cleaner/maintenance person on hand to help with properties when I was in a pinch. So, in 2016 I opened MACS Maintenance And Cleaning Services. Having my own maintenance and cleaning company has been extremely helpful in getting minor repairs and cleaning done on my rentals quickly, efficiently and affordably.

In my spare time - not that I have much - I enjoy doing anything and everything with my boys. I have been a team mom for about 20 years now in just about every sport - soccer, baseball, basketball, flag football, and tackle football. I volunteer much of my time to the Tucson Youth Football and Spirit Federation as I am a Board Member on the Wildcat Youth Football League. I have done everything from field setup, team mom, business manager, event assistance, fundraising, registration, field marshall, and I am currently responsible for running the snack bar!

about:
Assured Property Management

Our mission is twofold – coming together to meet the needs of all of our clients, while striving to elevate the industry’s standards. For property owners, we will bring positive change to the field of property management by providing commitment to service, knowledge through experience, the constant assessment of best practices and efficiencies, communication with clarity, and the delivery on all promises we make. For renters, we will connect those who are looking for their next home with our property owners’ quality properties, and do so in a manner that respects their wellbeing, follows the lease and law, and addresses their needs timely. No one should ever be made to feel as if they’re nothing more than a dollar sign. We want to share our passion for the field with everyone. Relationships matter.

Jamie Braun, owner and Property Manager in Charge of ASSURED Property Management, has been managing properties all throughout Horry County since early 2019. Prior to that, she spent a decade crusading for animal welfare in nonprofit development roles, and later transitioned to property and regime management in Charleston, SC. Jamie holds a graduate degree in Human Services and a Bachelors degree in Business Administration. She is professionally licensed in the state of SC and has completed several training programs in Fair Housing, Business and Process Management, HOA Management, Marketing, Operations, and Accounting. She is a self-admitted lifelong learner.

With a passion for real estate and homes, Jamie loves the variation of property management, in that there is always something new and different on a daily basis. She loves getting to work with owners, residents, and vendors, building strong relationships based on clarity and transparency. With her strategic mindset, she has helped many businesses grow and improve over the years, which led to her vision for how she wanted to develop and maintain her own management company. Promising to do things differently and to always operate with integrity, she hopes to make a name for herself by earning trust.

Note from Jamie: I started this company because it’s my passion and I saw a need for a management company that delivers on its promises. I’m not just in it for the money — I truly enjoy what I do and I’m selective with high standards. We must be a good fit for a trusting partnership. I strongly believe in quality over quantity. As a boutique management company, we limit our clients to ensure you always receive the high level of service promised from day one. You will never be just a number to me.

about:
Matthew was born in Chicago, and grew up in South Bend, Indiana. After relocating to Charlotte in 2005 while in Management for a Fortune 500 company, he decided that it was time to pursue a career in Real Estate. Matthew has been a Full Time Realtor & Broker since 2006, and also a Full Time Property Manager since 2009. He resides in the Charlotte Metro Area, with his wife Cynthia, their two children Sophia, and Matthew, Jr., and dog, Peterson. Matthew loves to call Charlotte home!

In his spare time, Matthew enjoys spending time with family, vacationing, pulling for the Charlotte Hornets, and volunteering. An advocate for homeless resources in Charlotte, Matthew volunteers with multiple organizations that deal with homelessness, as well as at Good Shepherd United Methodist Church. Matthew also serves on the Professional Standards Committee at the Canopy Realtor Association, on the MLS Advisory Committee at Canopy MLS, as Chapter President for the Charlotte Regional Chapter of the National Association of Residential Property Managers, on the Professional Development Committee, and the Member Services Committee for the National Association of Residential Property Managers . He is a member of the National Association of Realtors, North Carolina Association of Realtors, South Carolina Association of Realtors, Canopy Realtor Association, Piedmont Regional Association of Realtors, North Carolina Association of Realtors Property Management Division, and National Association of Residential Property Managers.

Born and raised in Hawaii, Jas has been a licensed real estate professional since 2007, specializing in both residential property management and sales. With nearly two decades of hands-on experience, she brings deep local knowledge and a strong commitment to delivering exceptional service.

Jas is an active member of the National Association of Residential Property Managers (NARPM), staying current with industry trends and best practices to ensure her clients receive the highest level of expertise.

A proud graduate of Aiea High School and Missouri Valley College, she holds a degree in Business Administration and Marketing. Her ongoing dedication to professional growth empowers her to guide clients with confidence, care, and integrity throughout every step of the real estate journey.

Outside of work, Jas finds her greatest joy in spending time with her two beautiful children. Family is at the heart of everything she doesand that same dedication shines through in the relationships she builds with her clients.

about:
I am the Southern California Broker for an ERA team that operates in California, Nevada, Utah, Arizona and Texas. Our organization transacts nearly $2 billion in real estate annually, and manage close to $500 million in real estate for our clients. We accomplish this through our proprietary software offerings that more effectively connects our clients to our nearly 800 affiliated agents and brokers. Through our brokerage sales and property management experience, we help train and develop real estate entrepreneurs to cascade our messaging to their friends, families and spheres.

We are passionate about peace - financial peace in particular. We believe it spreads through real estate more than any other investment. Our vocation is to educate and serve our clients so their real estate decisions bring them closer to reaching their lifelong goals. We love to partner with our clients and envision not the highest priced property attainable, but the smartest purchase that leads to less worry, more family-time, and more joy.

We believe that real estate ownership is the key to closing generational wealth gaps, and are relentless in our goals to coach and teach consumers and licensees alike in the opportunities that exist through real property ownership.

At Caspi Team Property Management, we run a full-service operation built for investors who want results without the stress. We handle every part of the rental cycle marketing, tenant screening, leasing, maintenance coordination, rent collection, inspections, and compliance always with an emphasis on maximizing ROI and minimizing risk.

Adam Kruse is the owner and broker of Hermann London Property Management, a St. Louis property management and real estate brokerage company serving rental property owners, real estate investors, tenants, buyers, and sellers throughout the St. Louis area. Hermann London provides professional property management services, leasing, tenant placement, rental property maintenance coordination, owner communication, accounting support, and residential real estate sales.

As both a real estate broker and investor, Adam understands the needs of rental property owners who want to protect their properties, reduce vacancy, place qualified tenants, improve cash flow, and build long-term real estate wealth. Hermann London Property Management works with single-family homes, condos,  multifamily properties, and investment properties across the greater St. Louis market.

The Hermann London team focuses on practical systems, responsive communication, strong leasing practices, maintenance oversight, and professional property management processes designed to help owners reduce stress and improve rental property performance. Whether an owner is looking for a St. Louis property manager, leasing support, rental property management, or investment real estate guidance, Hermann London is committed to helping clients make better real estate decisions and create a better experience for both owners and residents.

about:
Caryn Lynn Thomas
Service, professionalism and knowledge are keys to being successful.

Caryn began her career in real estate in 1994 as an administrative assistant and enjoyed the industry so much she became a licensed real estate agent. A few years after working as a part time agent, Caryn began working as a Realtor full time when she accepted a position with Jacksonvilles largest real estate corporation in their property management division. Caryn currently applies her knowledge and abilities as a property manager teaming up with Middleton Realty, Inc.

In addition to her work as a Realtor, Caryn is a member of the National Association of Residential Property Managers (NAPRM) and is Past President of NEFARPM, the local chapter, in 2006. Through this association she is able to keep abreast of local, state and national changes to legislation, marketing trends, and the local rental market.

When not active in real estate activities, Caryn enjoys traveling in her 73 Argosy by Airstream

After graduating from Fletcher Senior High and Florida Community College of Jacksonville, Caryn spent time as a young adult in Baltimore, Maryland, where she worked in the cemetery industry prior to returning to Jacksonville.

Amber Fuller-McDade thrives as a Multi-Business Owner, Realtor, Broker, Property Manager, General and Landscape Contractor, Author, Wife, Mother, and Boss. Amber is not your typical Real Estate Broker who is just trying to close a deal or manage a property. She is a profitable business owner in a career that she truly has a passion for.

Amber has always prioritized building strong client relationships and believes in educating clients to make informed decisions. Whether assisting a first-time buyer or an experienced investor, Amber looks at the whole picture to help her clients make the best decisions. Her true passion lies in creating "clients for life" and ensuring that every real estate endeavor is not only successful but also financially rewarding.

Above all, Amber believes that buying, selling, and investing in real estate will always be a smart investment. It's this conviction that drives her to stand by her clients, providing support and education throughout every step of the real estate journey.

about:
Not Your Run-of-the-Mill Property Manager!

Jose-Maria Chaparro or Cheppe as his friends like to call him,
became a California licensed Realtor in 2017 and is certified as a Condominium Specialist, always training and keeping up with the latest requirements of the field.
Cheppe is as conscientious as he is honorable. He started his career managing a Commercial Production Company focused on Latin American clients; he has worked with many international entertainment artists. In 2006, responding to the problems facing the homeless population in Los Angeles, he began working with the less fortunate. Jose Maria aided the L.A. Department of Mental Health and SHARE! to create and run the House with a Garden Project, one of the most successful solutions to date for homelessness. Cheppe holds a B.A. degree in Social Communication with a minor in Educational TV from Javeriana University, as well as an Associates degree in psychology from Los Angeles City College.

His commitment to provide best-practical solutions in Real Estate and Property Management for Residential and Commercial clients in California, in conjunction with his work at Jenkins Properties Management seals the deal!

about:
Brian Bean is a Rental Home Advocate, Property Manager & Realtor in the Inland Empire of Southern California. He moved from the Pacific Northwest in 1988 and worked as a newspaper editor for 13 years in Palm Springs. He bought his first apartment building in 1998 and learned property management in the trenches, later earning his real estate broker's license and opening his own company.

Brian and his brother Tim were raised by a single mother who worked three jobs simultaneously in order to save for a down payment on a house. When she bought her first home, it was life-altering for them all, and it significantly shaped Brian's personal mission: "Stable Home Life, Enduring Bonds, Strong Families." Today, he helps others manage their rental homes, with an emphasis on teaching his Investor clients to use the 5 Profit Centers to grow their rental portfolios into passive-income wealth-building machines.

He and wife Alisa have been married since 2002 and they love to scuba dive, kayak, and travel.

about:
Sam Eddinger has a passion for real estate with a mission to help anyone interested become successful in real estate. This has led Sam to start and partner on multiple companies including buy and hold investments with his wife and partners, flipping, and running a successful property management company. Sam is the founder of Ironclad Property Management, an investor focused property management company in central CT.

Sam has been involved in real estate from a young age, helping his mother and father maintain their real estate properties. Sam witnessed first hand how investing in real estate with a long time horizon can build wealth. With the real estate market near a bottom in 2012, Sam saw an opportunity and starting aggressively buying real estate in central Connecticut. In 2018, after 15 years as a nuclear engineer, he surprised his coworkers and managers by leaving his desk job to start Ironclad Property Management. Because of his experience in engineering, Sam brings a data analysis and process based approach to property management. Because of his experience as an investor, Sam works to help each client improve their investment rate of return by renting high, renting fast, and renting well.

For anyone that wants to be successful in real estate as a first time investor, or anyone tired being constantly inundated with complaints from residents, Sam is your guy to call.

about:
My name is Jason Hewitt, and I have lived and worked in the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex my entire life. My family and I live in Flower Mound, and I am very familiar with the Lewisville, Flower Mound, Carrollton, Farmers Branch, Coppell, Grapevine, Southlake, Las Colinas, and Irving areas, having either lived or worked in each of them as some point in my life.

Ive been with RE/MAX since 2009, earning the Rookie of the Year award my first year, RE/MAXs Executive Club in 2010 & 2011, and the prestigious RE/MAX 100% club each year since 2012. In 2018 I was named to the RE/MAX Hall of Fame and included in the Top 500 RE/MAX agents of Texas. I have always been an advocate of learning, and have taken classes and achieved the following certifications: Certified Residential Specialist (CRS), Accredited Buyers Specialist, E-Pro (Technology Specialist), Certified Home Marketing Specialist (Staging), International Real Estate Specialist, and Short Sale & Foreclosure Resource. I earned my Brokers License in the fall of 2015.

Real estate is my second profession. I was a teacher for 14 years. I taught choir in Carrollton and choir and theatre in Dallas. I was also the testing coordinator for the high school in Dallas, so details and accountability are right up my alley! My hobbies are volunteer work (usually with teenagers) and musical theatre! You can find me in a theatre audience at least 3 times a month. I also judge for the High School Musical Theatre Awards (through DSM) and Schmidt & Jones Theatre Awards.

Although I will always miss directing Opening Night, I enjoy matching both individuals and families with places they can make memories in and refer to as home.

about:
PMI North Atlanta is a locally, family owned and operated property management company serving residential property owners and real estate investors across North Atlanta. We help take the stress out of rental ownership by providing reliable, responsive support and a clear plan for protecting your property and maximizing its performance.

We know that for most owners, a rental property is more than just real estate. It is a major investment, a long-term financial tool, and something that needs to be cared for the right way. That is why we focus on strong communication, practical guidance, and day-to-day management that helps owners feel informed and confident.

Tyler Chenault leads leasing operations with a relationship-first approach and a strong understanding of what matters most to investors. He is known for being accessible, proactive, and committed to helping owners make wise decisions for their properties. His goal is simple: build trust, create value, and make sure clients feel supported every step of the way.

Whether you own one rental home or a growing portfolio, PMI North Atlanta brings together professional systems and personal service to help you move forward with confidence.

If you are looking for a property management team that is easy to work with, serious about protecting your investment, and ready to help, we would love to connect.

about: Kit started her real estate career 1980 after graduating from the University of Puget Sound. In the early 1980s she began working in the Relocation Division of MacPhersons Inc. Realtors. She began buying real estate in her twenties and has become a successful real estate investor. This knowledge in owning and managing properties is what Kit has based the foundation of Maple Leaf Management LLC on. She believes that property managers need to listen closely and carefully to their clients and speak honestly to set realistic expectations and to help make educated decisions. She has developed a team of professionals that are second to none in the area.

about:
Jennifer, a native of York County, South Carolina, boasts a rich background in property management cultivated since her high school graduation from Rock Hill High in 2012. Commencing her professional journey as a leasing consultant at a local apartment complex, Jennifer swiftly ascended to a pivotal role within the esteemed Carolinas Dynamic Realty team, where she has excelled since October of 2016.

Residing in Clover, South Carolina, alongside her husband, Patrick, their daughters, Harper and Henleigh, and their loyal companion, Blue, Jennifer epitomizes a consummate professional deeply rooted in her community. Beyond her professional pursuits, Jennifer embraces her passions, including football, travel, and cherished moments with her family.

Jennifer's dedication to her craft is underscored by her licensure in Real Estate for both North and South Carolina. Moreover, her commitment to excellence is mirrored in her affiliations with esteemed organizations, including the National Association of Residential Property Managers, National Association of Realtors, North Carolina Association of Realtors, and Canopy Realtor Association.

Notably, Jennifer contributes her expertise to the industry through her involvement in key roles, such as serving on the North Carolina Property Management Conference Planning Committee and holding the position of Treasurer for the Charlotte Regional Chapter of the National Association of Residential Property Managers. Previously, she assumed the role of Secretary within the same esteemed chapter, demonstrating her unwavering dedication to advancing the standards of professionalism within the field.

about: Anne Grimes is a licensed Real Estate Broker and owner of Stoneridge Realty and Property Management Services. about:
Eva grew up in Northeastern, NC and attended college at East Carolina University. In 2000, she took her first multifamily management position as a Leasing Agent with an apartment community in Charleston, SC. From there she escalated to Property Manager of a 400+ unit community, which moved her to Wilmington.

Eva now calls Wilmington home, after being here since 2002. Her passion for the coast, love for the sun and beaches has helped her fit right in. She has a passion for volunteering with her church and helping make an impact on the community. Her husband is a Wilmington native and they have been married for 20 years and have one son who aspires to have a career in automotive racing industry.

Eva left the multi-family industry after obtaining her NC Real Estate License in 2005 and moved into the Single Family Residential scene. This is where she flourished because she was challenged with a variety of properties, property owners and personalities - it was not all onsite and the same. Again moving her way up to Property Manager with a smaller local firm she learned and enhanced her leadership skills. After a few years of working for the smaller firm, she took another leap over to the largest property management firm in southeastern North Carolina. She worked her way from a Business Development Assistant to overseeing the Maintenance Department and finally took over the General Manager position. After 13 years, it was time for something different but wanting to stay in the property management industry, Eva took a position with a national vendor for property managers and worked remote. Now, she calls Unlimited Property Solutions home as Broker in Charge & General Manager, loves working back in the office with people and loves her team!

Hello, my name is Elias Camhi, I am the President/Broker of ELDA Management Services. I want to tell you a little bit about myself and how ELDA grew to become such an amazing company.

When I first moved to Houston from El Paso in 1992, I thought I hit the jackpot. I saw a city that was full of excitement and more importantly, there were trees. It was a city with potential to do anything and everything I wanted.

I didnt start in Property Management, but, in the IT world. Worked for various companies from small to large, including Compaq and later HP. I have done everything from testing equipment to writing programs that were utilized worldwide within HP.

During that time, I decided to start investing in real estate, thanks to the outstanding books written by Robert Kiyosaki. Soon we had a few properties, while our family and friends started doing the same and asking us to take care of theirs as well.

After the HP merger with Compaq, job loss and migration of jobs to China & India from one of the most beautiful campuses in Houston was beginning to take shape. I managed to continue working because of the programs I had written required constant maintenance. Needless to say, it would be a matter of time before I would be susceptible to losing my job. So, I completed my Masters degree in business, while working and supporting my family.

Shortly thereafter, I wrote the business plan to form a high tech property management company. In January 2004, I launched ELDA Management Services. I took a leap of faith and left HP to spend my full time growing ELDA. Founder of United Properties of West Michigan LLC, Certified Residential Management Professional (RMP) Owner/Broker of United Properties of West Michigan a Residential Property Management Company, Licensed Real Estate Broker, CE Instructor for Realtors, Past RPOA Board Member serving as treasurer and member of the Programs & Education Committee. Russ has 40+ years of experience as a Landlord.

He founded United Properties in 2005, which specializes in residential property management and remodeling services for national and international clients. He was the first person to receive the RMP designation from the National Association of Rental Property Management (NARPM) in western Michigan and one of only three in the entire state of Michigan. Russ's past experience includes wholesale distribution, retail, commercial development, financial business consulting, and business merger and acquisitions. Russ is a member of several associations including NARPM, RPOA, GRAR, MAR, NAR, NREIA. He attends national conventions with various associations each year to keep up on the latest technology, business strategies, and laws relating to residential property management.

Russ has been happily married for over 45 years, has 4 children and 13 grandchildren, 11 of which are living. Tim, his second son is now running the day-to-day operations of United Properties and the family business is currently being transitioned to Tim.

Chuck obtained his Residential Managment Professional (RMP) designation from the National Association of Residential Property Managers (NARPM) in 2017. This designation is only held by about 25 property managers in the State of Colorado.

Chuck grew up in Pagosa Springs, Colorado. In high school he was an active member of search and rescue. He joined the United States Marine Corps in 1999. As a Corporal, he was with the first troops entering Iraq in March of 2003. After his military service, he began a career in civil service. Chuck learned the value of helping people at an early age from his parents. Both worked in civil service; his mother as a dispatcher and his father as a police officer.

Chuck and his family have lived in the San Luis Valley since 2005. While living and working in the San Luis Valley, he attended Adams State College and obtained his degree in 2010. Family and career are Chucks priorities.
